title: Erdős-Hajnal Conjecture (Conjecture 1.1)
statement_text: For every graph $H$, there exists $c > 0$ such that every $H$-free graph $G$ satisfies $\alpha(G)\omega(G) \geq |G|^c$.
context_text: The Erdős-Hajnal conjecture is the central motivation for the study of pure pairs and the strong Erdős-Hajnal property. The paper proves bipartite analogues of the strong Erdős-Hajnal property for forest bigraphs, extending the pure-pairs programme introduced in earlier work in the series.
paper_contribution: Proves bipartite analogues of the strong Erdős-Hajnal property (Theorems 1.5 and 1.6), establishing that excluding a forest bigraph and its bicomplement forces linear-sized pure pairs in each part, and providing one-sided sparse versions; these results imply bipartite Erdős-Hajnal-type bounds.
notes: Labeled explicitly as 'Conjecture 1.1' in the paper with attribution to references [6,7]. The paper's central contribution is the bipartite analogue rather than a direct special case of the EH conjecture itself.
paper_title: Pure pairs. IV. Trees in bipartite graphs

title: Alecu-Atminas-Lozin-Zamaraev / Axenovich-Tompkins-Weber Bipartite Strong Erdős-Hajnal Conjecture
statement_text: For every forest bigraph $H$ with bicomplement $J$, there exists $\varepsilon > 0$ such that, if $G$ is a bigraph that is both $H$-free and $J$-free, then there is a pure pair $(Z_1, Z_2)$ in $G$ with $Z_i \subseteq V_i(G)$ and $|Z_i| \geq \varepsilon|V_i(G)|$ for $i = 1, 2$.
context_text: This conjecture posits the bipartite analogue of the strong Erdős-Hajnal property: excluding a forest bigraph and its bicomplement forces a linear-sized pure pair in each part of a bigraph. It was independently conjectured by Alecu, Atminas, Lozin and Zamaraev [1] and by Axenovich, Tompkins and Weber [3], and the paper states it is proved in full as the main result (Theorem 1.5).
attributed_to: Alecu, Atminas, Lozin, and Zamaraev; Axenovich, Tompkins, and Weber
paper_contribution: Proves the conjecture in full as the main result of the paper (Theorem 1.5), establishing the bipartite strong Erdős-Hajnal property for all forest bigraphs, and also proves one-sided sparse and not-very-dense variants (Theorems 1.6 and 1.7).
notes: The conjecture is not given a dedicated labeled theorem environment in the extracted text but is explicitly named as a conjecture of those authors and identified as the paper's main result.
paper_title: Pure pairs. IV. Trees in bipartite graphs

title: Korándi-Pach-Tomon Conjecture (ordered bigraphs)
statement_text: The statement of Theorem 1.7 still holds when $G$ and $H$ are ordered bigraphs (bigraphs $G$ with fixed linear orders on $V_1(G)$ and on $V_2(G)$) and containment is required to respect the linear orders: for every forest ordered bigraph $H$ and every $\tau$ with $0 < \tau \leq 1$, there exists $\varepsilon > 0$ such that if $G$ is an $H$-free ordered bigraph with at most $(1-\tau)|V_1(G)|\cdot|V_2(G)|$ edges, then there are anticomplete sets $Z_i \subseteq V_i(G)$ with $|Z_i| \geq \varepsilon|V_i(G)|$ for $i = 1, 2$.
context_text: Korándi, Pach and Tomon [10] made a much stronger conjecture than Theorem 1.7, asking that the sparse-bigraph result extend to the ordered setting (equivalently, to 0-1 matrices with the Zarankiewicz-type forbidden pattern condition). The paper explicitly notes this conjecture remains open and refers the reader to a companion paper [12] for further discussion.
paper_contribution: No progress is made on this conjecture in the paper; it is highlighted as an important open problem that goes beyond the main results.
notes: Statement reconstructed from prose description; not given in a labeled theorem environment. Described by the paper as 'much stronger' than Theorem 1.7 and explicitly noted to remain open.
paper_title: Pure pairs. IV. Trees in bipartite graphs
